# DeSlop

DeSlop is a deterministic repository scanner for visible evidence that software is unwired, overclaimed, or unfinished. It is built for public GitHub browser scans, local CLI audits, and CI quality gates.

Public page: https://deslop.imagineqira.com/
Repository: https://github.com/TheArtOfSound/DeSlop
Full context: https://deslop.imagineqira.com/llms-full.txt
Usage guide: https://deslop.imagineqira.com/USAGE.md
Status: https://deslop.imagineqira.com/status.json

## Core command

```bash
npx -y github:TheArtOfSound/DeSlop -- .
```

## Useful options

```bash
npx -y github:TheArtOfSound/DeSlop -- . --json
npx -y github:TheArtOfSound/DeSlop -- . --fail-on high
npx -y github:TheArtOfSound/DeSlop -- . --min-score 90
```

## What DeSlop checks

- Vague product copy and generic SaaS filler.
- Unsupported maturity claims.
- Weak error messages that do not explain cause, consequence, or next step.
- Dead navigation targets and fake-complete UI controls.
- Browser-side auth storage.
- Debug residue outside docs or visible code examples.
- Unimplemented branches and stubbed runtime paths.
- Unresolved work markers that should become issues, tests, or named non-goals.

## Report contract

- schemaVersion: 1
- summary score and counts
- category counts
- exact finding evidence
- file path, line, matched text, reason, and fix direction for each finding

## Rule categories

- copy
- ux
- implementation
- security
- release-hygiene

## Design constraint

DeSlop does not claim to identify whether code was written by a human or an AI model. It checks visible repository evidence and reports concrete findings with file, line, matched text, reason, and fix direction.
